title: Asking About Cooking Classes
summary: Maya visits a cooking school in Florence and asks the receptionist about the price, schedule, and what is included in the beginners' Italian cooking class.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Good afternoon. I saw your poster outside about cooking classes. Could you tell me a little more?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Of course. We have three levels: beginners, intermediate, and advanced. Which one are you interested in?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Beginners, definitely. I've cooked simple things at home, but I've never made fresh pasta or proper sauces.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Then the beginners' class is perfect. It runs every Tuesday and Saturday from four until seven in the afternoon.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: How many students join one class?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Eight, no more. The chef likes to give personal attention to everyone.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: That sounds great. What about the price?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: A single class is sixty-five euros. A package of four classes is two hundred and twenty euros, which is a bit cheaper.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Does the price include the food?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Yes, all ingredients, an apron, the printed recipes, and a glass of wine while you eat what you cook.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Lovely. Should I bring anything special?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Just comfortable shoes. The kitchen is warm, and you'll stand for most of the class.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Could I book a single class first, then decide if I want the package?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Of course. Would you like next Tuesday at four? You'll learn how to make fresh tagliatelle.

annotations.exampleEn: I saw the poster on the wall outside.
She signed up for cooking classes last month.
Fresh pasta tastes very different from dried pasta.

annotations.meaningVi: các loại nước sốt đúng kiểu
annotations.exampleEn: He learned to make proper sauces in Italy.

annotations.meaningVi: sự quan tâm dành riêng cho từng người

annotations.exampleEn: The four-class package is the best value.
All ingredients are included in the price.
Every student gets a clean apron.
We received the printed recipes after class.

annotations.meaningVi: mì sợi dẹt tagliatelle (loại pasta Ý)
annotations.exampleEn: We made fresh tagliatelle from scratch.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'never' for experience
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng have/has + never + V3 để nói rằng mình chưa từng làm điều gì trong đời. Trong bài: 'I've never made fresh pasta or proper sauces.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'have/has + never + past participle' to say you have not done something in your life so far. From the dialogue: 'I've never made fresh pasta or proper sauces.'

grammarNotes.title: Polite requests: 'Could you tell me…?' and 'Could I…?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'Could you + V?' để nhờ ai làm gì, và 'Could I + V?' để xin phép. Trong bài: 'Could you tell me a little more?' (nhờ giải thích) và 'Could I book a single class first?' (xin phép đặt).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Could you + verb?' for polite requests, and 'Could I + verb?' for asking permission. From the dialogue: 'Could you tell me a little more?' and 'Could I book a single class first?'

translationVi:
Maya: Chào chị. Tôi vừa thấy tờ áp phích bên ngoài về các lớp nấu ăn. Chị nói thêm cho tôi được không?
Receptionist: Tất nhiên rồi. Chúng tôi có ba cấp độ: cơ bản, trung cấp và nâng cao. Chị quan tâm cấp độ nào?
Maya: Chắc chắn là cơ bản. Tôi có nấu vài món đơn giản ở nhà, nhưng chưa bao giờ làm pasta tươi hay các loại sốt đúng kiểu.
Receptionist: Vậy lớp cơ bản rất phù hợp với chị. Lớp diễn ra mỗi thứ Ba và thứ Bảy, từ bốn giờ đến bảy giờ chiều.
Maya: Mỗi lớp có bao nhiêu học viên?
Receptionist: Tám người, không hơn. Bếp trưởng thích quan tâm từng người một.
Maya: Nghe tuyệt đấy. Giá cả thế nào ạ?
Receptionist: Một buổi lẻ là sáu mươi lăm euro. Gói bốn buổi là hai trăm hai mươi euro, rẻ hơn một chút.
Maya: Giá có bao gồm đồ ăn không?
Receptionist: Có ạ — tất cả nguyên liệu, một chiếc tạp dề, công thức in sẵn, và một ly rượu vang dùng kèm phần ăn chị tự nấu.
Maya: Tuyệt. Tôi có cần mang gì đặc biệt không?
Receptionist: Chỉ cần giày thoải mái. Bếp khá ấm, và chị sẽ đứng trong gần như cả buổi.
Maya: Tôi đặt một buổi trước, rồi quyết định có lấy gói cả khoá hay không, được không?
Receptionist: Tất nhiên. Chị muốn thứ Ba tới lúc bốn giờ chứ? Buổi đó chị sẽ học cách làm tagliatelle tươi.

questions.prompt: How did Maya hear about the classes?
questions.explanation: 'I saw your poster outside about cooking classes.'

questions.prompt: How many levels does the school offer?
questions.hint: Listen for the list of levels.

questions.prompt: What two things has Maya never made before?
questions.explanation: 'I've never made fresh pasta or proper sauces.'

questions.prompt: What days does the beginners' class run?
questions.explanation: 'every Tuesday and Saturday from four until seven in the afternoon.'

questions.prompt: How many students join one class?
questions.hint: Listen for the maximum number of students.

questions.prompt: How much does a single class cost?
questions.hint: Listen for the price of one class only.

questions.prompt: How much does the package of four classes cost?
questions.explanation: 'A package of four classes is two hundred and twenty euros.'

questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT included in the price?
options: A glass of wine with the meal
A bottle of water for the journey home
questions.explanation: Ingredients, apron, recipes, and a glass of wine are included; a bottle of water is not mentioned.
questions.hint: Listen for the list of included items.

questions.prompt: What does the receptionist tell Maya to bring?
questions.hint: Listen for what she should bring.

questions.prompt: What dish will Maya learn next Tuesday?
questions.explanation: 'You'll learn how to make fresh tagliatelle.'
questions.hint: Listen for an Italian pasta name.

cloze.template:
Maya: Good afternoon. I saw your {{b1}} outside about cooking classes. Could you tell me a little more?
Receptionist: Of course. We have three {{b2}}: beginners, intermediate, and advanced. Which one are you interested in?
Maya: Beginners, definitely. I've cooked simple things at home, but I've {{b3}} made fresh pasta or proper sauces.
Receptionist: Then the beginners' class is perfect. It {{b4}} every Tuesday and Saturday from four until seven in the afternoon.
Maya: How many students join one class?
Receptionist: Eight, no more. The chef likes to give personal {{b5}} to everyone.
Maya: That sounds great. What about the {{b6}}?
Receptionist: A single class is sixty-five euros. A package of four classes is two hundred and twenty euros, which is a bit cheaper.
Maya: Does the price include the food?
Receptionist: Yes, all {{b7}}, an apron, the printed recipes, and a glass of wine while you eat what you cook.
Maya: Lovely. Should I bring anything special?
Receptionist: Just comfortable {{b8}}. The kitchen is warm, and you'll stand for most of the class.
Maya: Could I {{b9}} a single class first, then decide if I want the package?
Receptionist: Of course. Would you like next Tuesday at four? You'll learn how to make fresh {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'poster' — a large advertisement on a wall.
'never' — she has not done this before.
'runs' — a class that 'runs' takes place regularly.
'shoes' — because she will stand a lot.
'book' — to reserve a place in a class.
'tagliatelle' — the specific dish for next Tuesday's class.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Cooking classes in Florence often cost more than a restaurant meal. Do you think learning to make a dish yourself is worth more than just eating it? In what way does cooking change how we feel about food?
